doc.advanced_input_desc: Used for special input formats such as file uploads, complex selections, and ratings.
doc.calendar_a11y_desc: Uses role='grid' with proper aria-label. Date cells are navigable with arrow keys. Selected date is announced with aria-selected.
doc.calendar_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.calendar_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.calendar_best_practice_1: Use Calendar consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.calendar_best_practice_2: Test Calendar behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.calendar_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like DatePicker better suits the use case.
doc.calendar_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.calendar_choice_self_when: When Calendar functionality with standard options is required.
doc.calendar_content_guideline: Clearly indicate today's date and the selected date with distinct visual styles.
doc.calendar_design_intent: Calendar provides a standalone date selection interface, allowing users to view and pick dates from a monthly grid without a text input field.
doc.calendar_motion_desc: Month transitions use a smooth slide animation to maintain spatial context.
doc.calendar_scenario_1: Integrating Calendar into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.calendar_scenario_2: Using Calendar in management views for information organization.
doc.cascader_a11y_desc: Supports keyboard navigation between levels with arrow keys. Each level panel is announced via aria-live.
doc.cascader_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.cascader_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.cascader_best_practice_1: Use Cascader consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.cascader_best_practice_2: Test Cascader behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.cascader_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like TreeSelect better suits the use case.
doc.cascader_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.cascader_choice_self_when: When Cascader functionality with standard options is required.
doc.cascader_content_guideline: Keep hierarchy depths reasonable (3-4 levels max). Provide search functionality for large datasets.
doc.cascader_design_intent: Cascader provides a multi-level dropdown for selecting from hierarchical data, revealing child options progressively as parent categories are chosen.
doc.cascader_hover_expand_desc: By specifying `expandTrigger="hover"`, submenus can be expanded by hovering instead of clicking.
doc.cascader_long_desc: Cascader is used to navigate through a multi-level menu to drill down and select a final value.
doc.cascader_motion_desc: Sub-menus slide in from the side as each level is selected, providing clear spatial hierarchy.
doc.cascader_scenario_1: Integrating Cascader into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.cascader_scenario_2: Using Cascader in management views for information organization.
doc.cascader_title: A component for selecting a single value by navigating through hierarchical data.
doc.checkbox_a11y_keyboard: Toggle the checked state using the Space key.
doc.checkbox_a11y_label: Explicitly link label text with the input so it can be announced by screen readers.
doc.checkbox_anatomy_box: A custom visual part that represents the checked state.
doc.checkbox_anatomy_input: A hidden element that holds the actual checked state.
doc.checkbox_anatomy_label: A clickable text area that enhances affordance.
doc.checkbox_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Checkbox component. Use standalone or with CheckboxGroup for multiple selections.
doc.checkbox_choice_check_when: When multiple answers are possible, or for a single consent item.
doc.checkbox_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the most appropriate component based on the number and nature of choices.
doc.checkbox_choice_radio_when: When exactly one option must be selected from multiple choices.
doc.checkbox_choice_switch_when: When toggling a feature on or off results in an immediate change.
doc.checkbox_content_no_period: Avoid putting a period at the end of the labels.
doc.checkbox_content_positive: Use positive phrasing for labels (e.g., "Enable Notifications").
doc.checkbox_design_intent: Checkboxes are used to allow users to select multiple items from a list, or to toggle individual options (such as consent) on or off. The goal is to provide a clear indication of selection.
doc.checkbox_long_desc: Details and usage for the Checkbox component.
doc.checkbox_motion_check: Apply an animation where the checkmark appears to be drawn, providing a sense of achievement.
doc.checkbox_note_default: Generally, the default state should be unselected (off). This is crucial for user consent.
doc.checkbox_note_layout: Stack vertically and ensure a large tap area.
doc.checkbox_scenario_agreement: Terms of Service Consent: Placed as a required item before form submission.
doc.checkbox_scenario_filter: Search Filters: Selecting multiple categories to narrow down results.
doc.checkbox_scenario_newsletter: Notification Settings: Selecting multiple categories of information to receive in user profiles or settings.
doc.checkbox_state_indeterminate_desc: An intermediate state used when only some child elements are selected, such as in a 'Select All' checkbox.
doc.checkbox_when_to_use: Best for selecting multiple options or for Yes/No confirmations (e.g., terms of service consent).
doc.checkboxgroup_a11y_fieldset: Internally use <fieldset> and <legend> (or set appropriate WAI-ARIA attributes) to communicate to assistive technologies that these are a group of answers to a single question.
doc.checkboxgroup_anatomy_items: Individual options lined up within the group.
doc.checkboxgroup_anatomy_label: Explicitly states what the group is for selecting (e.g., 'Fields of Interest').
doc.checkboxgroup_best_practice_1: Always provide a group label (via Fieldset/legend or aria-labelledby) so screen readers can announce the purpose of the group.
doc.checkboxgroup_best_practice_2: Keep the option list short (≤8 items). For larger sets, prefer MultiSelect to avoid overwhelming the user.
doc.checkboxgroup_choice_group_when: When users select multiple options from a short visible list (≤8 items) and all options should be visible at once.
doc.checkboxgroup_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the right multi-selection component for the context.
doc.checkboxgroup_choice_multiselect_when: When the option list is long (>8 items) or space is constrained, and users benefit from search/filter functionality.
doc.checkboxgroup_content_sort: If there are many choices, list them in an order that is easy for users to find, such as alphabetical or by frequency of use.
doc.checkboxgroup_design_intent: CheckboxGroup is a component for organizing related multiple checkboxes as a logical group and managing a common state (an array of values). It provides a consistent layout and operability in scenarios where users need to select multiple options at once.
doc.checkboxgroup_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Checkbox Group component.
doc.checkboxgroup_motion_stagger: When lists are displayed dynamically, items appear sequentially and softly to visually communicate the grouping of information.
doc.checkboxgroup_scenario_filter: Search Filter: Specifying multiple product categories or brands.
doc.checkboxgroup_scenario_hobby: User Profile: Multiple selection of hobbies or special skills.
doc.checkboxgroup_title: Management component for grouping multiple checkboxes
doc.colorinput_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.colorinput_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.colorinput_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.colorinput_best_practice_1: Use ColorInput consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.colorinput_best_practice_2: Test ColorInput behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.colorinput_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like ColorPicker better suits the use case.
doc.colorinput_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.colorinput_choice_self_when: When ColorInput functionality with standard options is required.
doc.colorinput_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.colorinput_design_intent: ColorInput provides a consistent, reusable pattern for text-based color value input with preview, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.colorinput_long_desc: A component that provides a color input.
doc.colorinput_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.colorinput_scenario_1: Integrating ColorInput into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.colorinput_scenario_2: Using ColorInput in management views for information organization.
doc.colorinput_title: Field for selecting colors and entering hex codes with preview
doc.colorpicker_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.colorpicker_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.colorpicker_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.colorpicker_best_practice_1: Use ColorPicker consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.colorpicker_best_practice_2: Test ColorPicker behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.colorpicker_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like ColorInput better suits the use case.
doc.colorpicker_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.colorpicker_choice_self_when: When ColorPicker functionality with standard options is required.
doc.colorpicker_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.colorpicker_design_intent: ColorPicker provides a consistent, reusable pattern for visual color selection with spectrum and sliders, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.colorpicker_long_desc: A component for users to pick colors. It wraps the native input type="color" and adjusts its styles.
doc.colorpicker_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.colorpicker_scenario_1: Integrating ColorPicker into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.colorpicker_scenario_2: Using ColorPicker in management views for information organization.
doc.colorpicker_title: Component for choosing colors from a visual palette
doc.combobox_a11y_autocomplete: Properly set aria-autocomplete to communicate to screen readers that the list changes with input.
doc.combobox_anatomy_field: The area for entering search queries or free description.
doc.combobox_anatomy_list: A menu that displays candidates filtered based on the entered content.
doc.combobox_best_practice_1: Always provide an empty-state message (e.g., 'No results found') when filtering returns no matches, so users understand the search worked but yielded nothing.
doc.combobox_best_practice_2: Debounce async data-fetching triggers to avoid flooding the backend with requests on every keystroke.
doc.combobox_choice_combobox_when: When there are a very large number of choices (100 or more), or when you want to allow custom values not in the list to be entered.
doc.combobox_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the number of choices and the need for free-form entry.
doc.combobox_choice_selectbox_when: When you want to restrict selection to only predefined lists.
doc.combobox_content_no_results: Display appropriate messages such as 'Not found' when there are no search results, suggesting the next action (e.g., create new).
doc.combobox_design_intent: Combobox is a component that combines the advantages of traditional Selectbox (selection) and Input (free-form entry). It provides high flexibility, such as performing filtering searches from a vast number of choices and allowing entry of new values not in the list as needed.
doc.combobox_long_desc: A combobox is an input component that allows the user to select options from a list or filter by typing text.
doc.combobox_motion_filter: The list is filtered smoothly with each input, visually communicating the narrowing down of results.
doc.combobox_scenario_address: Address Entry: Completion for prefectures or municipalities.
doc.combobox_scenario_tag: Tag Management: Selecting from existing tags or creating and entering new ones.
doc.combobox_title: Select box with search and autocomplete functionality
doc.datepicker_a11y_2: `aria-label` or `aria-describedby` is set for the input field.
doc.datepicker_a11y_4: Disabled dates are explicitly marked with aria-disabled.
doc.datepicker_a11y_desc: The calendar popup uses role='dialog' with aria-label. Date cells are navigable with arrow keys. Selected date is announced with aria-selected.
doc.datepicker_a11y_keyboard: Navigate through dates in the calendar with arrow keys and make selection possible with the Enter key. Also, allow closing the calendar with the Escape key.
doc.datepicker_adv_1: Intuitive date entry without needing specific calendar knowledge.
doc.datepicker_adv_2: Visual confirmation of dates via the calendar UI.
doc.datepicker_adv_3: Automatically prevents format errors during entry.
doc.datepicker_adv_4: Full support for accessible keyboard navigation.
doc.datepicker_anatomy_input: A field that displays the currently selected date and accepts keyboard input.
doc.datepicker_anatomy_popup: A floating panel for visually listing and selecting dates.
doc.datepicker_best_practice_1: Use clear date format labels (e.g., 'MM/DD/YYYY') so users know the expected input format.
doc.datepicker_best_practice_2: Provide both keyboard input and calendar picker to accommodate different user preferences.
doc.datepicker_choice_input_when: When entering a date the user already remembers quickly (e.g., date of birth, expiration date).
doc.datepicker_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal entry method depending on the nature of the date to be entered.
doc.datepicker_choice_picker_when: When it's necessary to select while checking the day of the week or availability (e.g., reservation date, event date).
doc.datepicker_clear_btn_desc: Optionally display a button to instantly clear the selected date.
doc.datepicker_content_format: Display the appropriate date format (YYYY/MM/DD, etc.) for the user's locale and communicate entry examples through placeholders.
doc.datepicker_control_types_desc: DatePicker supports both controlled and uncontrolled patterns.
doc.datepicker_custom_format_desc: Control the display format in the input field using the format property.
doc.datepicker_custom_popup_desc: Customize the content of the calendar popup using available slots.
doc.datepicker_design_intent: DatePicker is a component designed to allow users to intuitively select dates through a calendar-format UI. By being able to see the sense of the day of the week and specific periods (yesterday, tomorrow, etc.) while selecting, it prevents entry errors and reduces the user's memory load.
doc.datepicker_flow_1: Click the first time to select the start date.
doc.datepicker_flow_2: Click the second time to select the end date.
doc.datepicker_flow_3: The range between the start and end dates is highlighted during selection.
doc.datepicker_flow_4: If a date earlier than the start date is selected as the end date, they are automatically swapped.
doc.datepicker_guideline_1: Use when users need to select a specific date or ranges with visual calendar confirmation.
doc.datepicker_guideline_2: For dates users know by heart (e.g., birth dates, expiry dates), direct text input is often more efficient.
doc.datepicker_integration_desc: DatePicker utilizes the shared Calendar component internally. See the Calendar documentation for more details.
doc.datepicker_kb_1: Press Enter or Space on the input field to open the calendar.
doc.datepicker_kb_2: Navigate through dates with arrow keys and press Enter to select.
doc.datepicker_kb_3: Close the calendar anytime by pressing the Escape key.
doc.datepicker_long_desc: Details and usage for the Date Picker component.
doc.datepicker_motion_fade: Add light fade and slide effects when the popup appears to clarify where the calendar is appearing from.
doc.datepicker_ts_desc: The DatePicker component provides comprehensive type definitions for safe usage.
doc.daterangepicker_a11y_desc: Both start and end date inputs are keyboard-accessible. The calendar popup announces the selected range via aria-live updates.
doc.daterangepicker_best_practice_1: Show a visual preview of the selected range between start and end dates in the calendar.
doc.daterangepicker_best_practice_2: Validate that the end date is not before the start date and provide clear error feedback.
doc.daterangepicker_choice_alt_when: When only a single date is needed (use DatePicker instead).
doc.daterangepicker_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the type of date selection needed.
doc.daterangepicker_choice_self_when: When users need to select a start and end date as a range (e.g., booking dates, report periods).
doc.daterangepicker_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the daterangepicker component.
doc.daterangepicker_title: A component for selecting a specific date range (start and end dates).
doc.dialog_best_practice_1: Use a Dialog only for critical interactions that require the user's full attention. For non-critical information, prefer Popover or Toast.
doc.dialog_best_practice_2: Always provide a clearly labeled close action (an X button or a 'Cancel' button) so users can dismiss the Dialog without completing the action.
doc.dropzone_a11y_desc: The dropzone area should have role='button' and respond to keyboard activation. Announce file acceptance or rejection via aria-live.
doc.dropzone_a11y_fallback: Always provide an alternative means of opening a file browser by clicking for users who have difficulty with drag and drop.
doc.dropzone_accept_desc: You can use the accept property to limit the file formats that can be uploaded.
doc.dropzone_anatomy_area: A large area surrounded by a border for dropping files into.
doc.dropzone_anatomy_text: Instructional messages such as 'Drop files here'.
doc.dropzone_best_practice_1: Show accepted file types and size limits clearly within the dropzone area.
doc.dropzone_best_practice_2: Provide visual feedback (border color change, icon) when files are dragged over the zone.
doc.dropzone_choice_alt_when: When a compact file input button is sufficient (use FileUpload).
doc.dropzone_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on how users should upload files.
doc.dropzone_choice_self_when: When you want a large drag-and-drop target area for intuitive file uploads.
doc.dropzone_content_limit: Clearly state the permitted file formats and maximum size so that users can make a judgment before uploading.
doc.dropzone_design_intent: Dropzone is a component designed to allow users to intuitively upload files. It improves the speed and experience of the operation by providing a 'large container' where users can drag and drop directly from the desktop, saving the trouble of searching through a file selection dialog.
doc.dropzone_long_desc: A component that supports file uploads via drag and drop.
doc.dropzone_motion_active: The background color or border changes when a file enters the area (Drag Over), providing visual feedback that it's ready for acceptance.
doc.dropzone_multiple_desc: By setting the multiple property to true, you can select and drop multiple files.
doc.dropzone_title: Area for uploading files via drag and drop
doc.fieldLabelContent_desc: Label text combined with required/optional badge
doc.fieldLabelContent_long_desc: FieldLabelContent renders the label text along with optional required or optional status badges.
doc.fieldLabelContent_summary: Component for displaying label text and required/optional badges
doc.fieldLabelContent_usage_desc: Used inside FieldTemplate to render the label area with required/optional indicators.
doc.fieldTemplate_a11y_desc: Automatically links the label to the input via htmlFor/id, and the error message via aria-describedby. The required/optional badge is hidden from screen readers.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_content_desc: The slot where the actual input component (Input, Selectbox, etc.) is placed.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_error_desc: Displays a validation error message below the input when the field is in an error state.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_label_desc: The text label displayed above (or beside) the input, linked via htmlFor.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_required_desc: A small badge indicating whether the field is required or optional.
doc.fieldTemplate_best_practice_1: Always pass an id to the input child that matches the label's htmlFor to ensure correct accessibility association.
doc.fieldTemplate_best_practice_2: Use the direction='horizontal' prop for settings forms where space allows, but test responsiveness on narrow screens.
doc.fieldTemplate_choice_alt_when: When only a styled input shell is needed without a label — use InputBase instead.
doc.fieldTemplate_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on whether you need a full form field or only the input shell.
doc.fieldTemplate_choice_use_when: When the input needs a visible label, required/optional badge, and error message.
doc.fieldTemplate_content_guideline_1: Always provide a meaningful label text — do not rely on placeholder text alone.
doc.fieldTemplate_content_guideline_2: Keep error messages concise and actionable, describing what the user needs to fix.
doc.fieldTemplate_desc: Common layout (label, error) for form fields
doc.fieldTemplate_design_intent: FieldTemplate standardizes the layout of form fields by composing a label area, an input slot, and an error region into a single reusable wrapper, ensuring consistency across all forms in the application.
doc.fieldTemplate_long_desc: FieldTemplate provides the standard layout wrapper for form fields, combining the label, optional/required badge, input slot, and error message in a consistent structure.
doc.fieldTemplate_motion_desc: Error messages appear with a subtle fade-in transition to draw attention without being disruptive.
doc.fieldTemplate_summary: Common layout for form fields (labels, error display)
doc.fieldTemplate_usage_desc: Wraps any form input to provide consistent label positioning, error display, and accessibility association.
doc.fieldTemplate_keyboard_desc: This component itself doesn't have specific keyboard interactions, but it ensures that the nested input field is correctly associated with its label and error message for assistive technologies.
doc.fieldTemplate_i18n_desc: All labels and error messages should be passed as translated strings. The component also supports localized 'Required' and 'Optional' labels.
doc.fieldTemplate_tokens_desc: Uses standard typography tokens for labels and error messages, and spacing tokens for consistent layout.
doc.fieldTemplate_responsive_desc: Supports both vertical and horizontal layouts. In horizontal mode, it automatically stacks vertically on smaller screens.
doc.fieldTemplate_scenario_1: Wrapping any input component in a form to provide consistent label and error layout.
doc.fieldTemplate_scenario_2: Used in settings panels where inputs need inline labels with horizontal layout.
doc.fielderror_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation used to display validation error messages below input fields.
doc.fielderror_long_desc: Details and usage for the Field Error component.
doc.fieldset_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation for grouping related form elements with a legend.
doc.fieldset_disabled_desc: Disables all nested form elements at once using the disabled property.
doc.fieldset_long_desc: Details and usage for the Fieldset component.
doc.fieldset_title: A container used to group related elements in a form, often with a legend.
doc.fieldset_with_legend_desc: Use the Fieldset.Legend subcomponent to provide a clear title for the group.
doc.fileupload_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.fileupload_accept_desc: You can limit the types of files (MIME types, extensions) that can be selected using the `accept` property.
doc.fileupload_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.fileupload_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.fileupload_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation allowing users to select or drag and drop files.
doc.fileupload_best_practice_1: Use FileUpload consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.fileupload_best_practice_2: Test FileUpload behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.fileupload_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Dropzone better suits the use case.
doc.fileupload_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.fileupload_choice_self_when: When FileUpload functionality with standard options is required.
doc.fileupload_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.fileupload_design_intent: FileUpload provides a consistent, reusable pattern for file selection via button click with upload management, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.fileupload_long_desc: Details and usage for the File Upload component.
doc.fileupload_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.fileupload_multi_file_desc: Enable multiple file selection by setting the multiple property.
doc.fileupload_multiple_desc: Setting the `multiple` property allows selecting multiple files simultaneously.
doc.fileupload_restricted_desc: Limit selectable files to specific formats (e.g., images only) using the accept property.
doc.fileupload_scenario_1: Integrating FileUpload into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.fileupload_scenario_2: Using FileUpload in management views for information organization.
doc.fileupload_usage_desc: Files can be uploaded by dragging & dropping or clicking to select.
doc.fileupload_with_icon_desc: An example of a customized upload area containing an icon.
doc.indicatorSlider_desc: Sliding highlight indicator for Tabs, SegmentedControl, etc.
doc.indicatorSlider_long_desc: IndicatorSlider is an animated sliding highlight component that tracks an active element and smoothly moves to indicate the current selection.
doc.indicatorSlider_summary: Sliding indicator for Tabs, SegmentedControl, etc.
doc.indicatorSlider_usage_desc: Used internally by Tabs and SegmentedControl to display an animated active indicator.
doc.inputBase_a11y_desc: Relies on the consuming component to provide aria-label, aria-describedby, and aria-invalid. InputBase itself is a visual container only.
doc.inputBase_anatomy_content_desc: The area where the actual input element or custom content is rendered.
doc.inputBase_anatomy_root_desc: The outermost wrapper providing border, background, and status styling.
doc.inputBase_best_practice_1: Never use InputBase directly in application code — use the higher-level components (Input, Selectbox, etc.) instead.
doc.inputBase_best_practice_2: If you need custom content inside a standard-looking input, prefer InputBase over writing custom border/focus styles.
doc.inputBase_choice_alt_when: When the input needs a label, required/optional badge, and error message — use FieldTemplate instead.
doc.inputBase_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on whether you need a styled container only or a full form field with label and error.
doc.inputBase_choice_use_when: When you need only the visual shell (border, icons, states) without a label or error message.
doc.inputBase_content_guideline_1: Do not place labels or error messages inside InputBase — use FieldTemplate for that.
doc.inputBase_content_guideline_2: Limit right icons to 2-3 at most to avoid cluttering the input area.
doc.inputBase_desc: Shared shell wrapper for Input, Selectbox, etc. (icons, clear button)
doc.inputBase_design_intent: InputBase serves as the structural shell for all text-input-like components. It enforces consistent icon placement, border treatment, and status styling (error, disabled) across Input, SearchInput, Selectbox, and similar components.
doc.inputBase_i18n_desc: The clearAriaLabel and leftIconAriaLabel props should be translated strings for screen reader support.
doc.inputBase_keyboard_desc: Keyboard interactions are delegated to the child input element. InputBase itself has no keyboard behavior.
doc.inputBase_long_desc: InputBase is the shared shell wrapper for input components. It provides a consistent container with optional icons, status styles (error, disabled), and action buttons (clear).
doc.inputBase_motion_desc: State transitions (hover, focus, error) use CSS transitions for smooth visual feedback.
doc.inputBase_responsive_desc: Respects the width prop (xs–xl or custom value) and supports fullWidth for fluid layouts.
doc.inputBase_scenario_1: Wrapping a custom date picker trigger to get consistent border and icon treatment.
doc.inputBase_scenario_2: Building a tag input where custom content needs the standard input shell styling.
doc.inputBase_summary: Shared shell component for Input, Selectbox, etc. (icons, clear button)
doc.inputBase_tokens_desc: Uses --wim-color-border-default, --wim-color-bg-input, and intent-based color tokens for consistent theming.
doc.inputBase_usage_desc: Used internally by Input, Selectbox, Combobox, SearchInput and others to ensure a consistent visual and functional shell.
doc.input_a11y_aria: When an error occurs, use aria-invalid="true" and aria-describedby to explicitly link it with the error message.
doc.input_a11y_labels: Link a <label> to every input field and do not rely solely on placeholders.
doc.input_anatomy_container: Forms the border and background, clarifying the input area.
doc.input_anatomy_field: The <input> element where the user actually types text.
doc.input_anatomy_icons: Places for search icons, clear buttons, etc. (Prefix/Suffix).
doc.input_basic_desc: Standard implementation of the text input field for user data entry.
doc.input_best_practice_1: Keep the number of Input fields per form section to a minimum; group related fields and consider multi-step patterns for long forms.
doc.input_best_practice_2: Always pair an Input with a visible label — never rely solely on placeholder text as a substitute for a label.
doc.input_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on the amount and nature of the information to be entered.
doc.input_choice_number_when: Suitable for numeric-only input (including increment/decrement operations) such as quantities or amounts.
doc.input_choice_textarea_when: Suitable for long, multi-line text or comments.
doc.input_clearable_desc: Example displaying a button to clear input content.
doc.input_content_labels: Keep labels short and clear, prioritizing item format like 'Name' over question format.
doc.input_content_placeholder: Use placeholders for input examples (e.g., John Doe) and do not use them as a replacement for labels.
doc.input_desc: Single-line input fields for text, numbers, or emails
doc.input_design_intent: Inputs (text fields) are the most basic components for obtaining short, free-form text information from users, such as names, addresses, and email addresses.
doc.input_ghost_desc: A simple style without a background color. It doesn’t emphasize the field normally, providing visual feedback only on hover or focus.
doc.input_ghost_i1: Empty: Placeholder suggests it is an input area
doc.input_ghost_i2: Hover: Displays a light background to notify it is intractable
doc.input_ghost_i3: Focus: Displays a primary-colored border to clearly show input state
doc.input_ghost_u1: Click-to-Edit UIs for titles on detail screens where display is primary
doc.input_ghost_u2: Noise reduction in screens with many input items like lists or tables
doc.input_ghost_u3: When you want to blend the search bar into the background
doc.input_long_desc: Details and usage for the Input component.
doc.input_motion_focus: The border color changes smoothly (transition) on focus to indicate that it is active.
doc.input_outline_desc: Standard style with a border (same as default, but used when specifying explicitly).
doc.input_scenario_form: Contact Form: Entering various attribute information.
doc.input_scenario_search: Search Bar: Entering single keywords for site-wide search.
doc.input_title: A standard field for users to enter short text freely
doc.input_variant_desc: You can change the style with the `variant` property.
doc.input_width_desc: By setting the `width` according to the length of the input content, you can visually convey to the user "what should be entered" (affordance). WIM UI provides semantic sizes (recommended) from `xs` to `xl`, which are responsive (automatically shrink to full width on smartphones).
doc.input_width_note: * Note that the `ch` unit is based on the width of the half-width digit "0", so when entering full-width characters (Japanese) or wide characters, the number of displayed characters may be fewer than expected. For multi-language support or general form design, the use of semantic sizes (`sm`, `md`, etc.) is recommended.
doc.input_width_usage: For detailed design guidelines, please also refer to the [Form UI Guide](/docs/guides-form-ui--docs#appropriate-width-setting-for-input-fields).
doc.inputgroup_basic_desc: Example adding a prefix to an Input using InputGroupText.
doc.inputgroup_fullwidth_desc: Using the fullWidth property expands it to the container width.
doc.inputgroup_long_desc: The InputGroup component is used to visually unify an Input with buttons or text (addons).
doc.inputgroup_multi_desc: Multiple InputGroupText components can be lined up.
doc.inputgroup_note: InputGroupText is for displaying information like units or prefixes. If clickable actions are needed, combine with the Button component.
doc.inputgroup_suffix_desc: Text addons can be added after the Input.
doc.inputgroup_with_button_desc: Action buttons can be placed next to the Input.
doc.inputmask_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.inputmask_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.inputmask_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.inputmask_basic_desc: Specify a pattern in the mask property.
doc.inputmask_best_practice_1: Use InputMask consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.inputmask_best_practice_2: Test InputMask behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.inputmask_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Input better suits the use case.
doc.inputmask_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.inputmask_choice_self_when: When InputMask functionality with standard options is required.
doc.inputmask_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.inputmask_design_intent: InputMask provides a consistent, reusable pattern for formatted text input with pattern enforcement (phone, SSN, etc.), reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.inputmask_long_desc: An input component with a mask to enforce input following a specific format.
doc.inputmask_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.inputmask_scenario_1: Integrating InputMask into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.inputmask_scenario_2: Using InputMask in management views for information organization.
doc.label_desc: Descriptive text for input fields, including required/optional marks
doc.label_long_desc: A component for displaying descriptions or captions for form elements.
doc.label_req_opt_desc: Use the equired property for a required mark and showOptional for an optional mark.
doc.label_title: Descriptive text for input fields, including required/optional marks
doc.legend_long_desc: The Legend component is used to display a group title within a Fieldset.
doc.mentions_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.mentions_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.mentions_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.mentions_best_practice_1: Use Mentions consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.mentions_best_practice_2: Test Mentions behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.mentions_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Combobox better suits the use case.
doc.mentions_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.mentions_choice_self_when: When Mentions functionality with standard options is required.
doc.mentions_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.mentions_design_intent: Mentions provides a consistent, reusable pattern for text input with @mention autocomplete for users or tags, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.mentions_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.mentions_scenario_1: Integrating Mentions into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.mentions_scenario_2: Using Mentions in management views for information organization.
doc.multiselect_a11y_announcement: Notify screen readers of the current selection status when items are added or deleted.
doc.multiselect_anatomy_tags: Display selected items in a chip format. Can be deleted individually with an X mark.
doc.multiselect_best_practice_1: Set a reasonable maxItems limit or paginate the dropdown to prevent performance degradation with very large option sets.
doc.multiselect_best_practice_2: Show selected item count or a summary tag when the selection overflows the visible field, so users always know how many items are selected.
doc.multiselect_choice_checkbox_when: When there are few choices (around 5) and you want all choices to be verifiable at a glance.
doc.multiselect_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the number of choices and the priority of the operation.
doc.multiselect_choice_multi_when: When there are many choices (10 or more) and you want to display the selected content compactly.
doc.multiselect_content_search: By enabling the search function, you can find the desired item quickly from a large list.
doc.multiselect_design_intent: MultiSelect is a component for users to efficiently select multiple items from a large number of choices. Selected items are displayed as 'tags' within the field, designed to allow easy verification and deletion of content.
doc.multiselect_long_desc: A dropdown menu for users to select multiple options from a predefined list.
doc.multiselect_motion_tag: When an item is selected, a tag appears softly, visually connecting that it has moved from the list to the field.
doc.multiselect_scenario_admin: Permission Management: Assigning multiple roles to a user.
doc.multiselect_scenario_skill: User Settings: Selecting skills or categories of interest.
doc.numberinput_a11y_stepper: Assign appropriate aria-labels to the spin buttons so their function can be understood by screen readers.
doc.numberinput_anatomy_input: An <input> element that accepts only numerical values.
doc.numberinput_anatomy_steppers: Up and down buttons for increasing or decreasing the value by a certain amount.
doc.numberinput_best_practice_1: Always define min, max, and step to prevent invalid out-of-range values and to convey the expected input range to the user.
doc.numberinput_best_practice_2: Display a unit label (e.g., 'kg', '%', '$') adjacent to the field so users understand the expected value format without guessing.
doc.numberinput_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the nature of the value to be entered and the frequency of adjustment.
doc.numberinput_choice_number_when: When continuous increment/decrement operations of numerical values such as quantity, age, or price are expected.
doc.numberinput_choice_slider_when: When intuitive proportions or sensory adjustments within a range are prioritized over precise numerical values.
doc.numberinput_choice_text_when: For 'numbers' with a strong symbolic meaning, such as phone numbers, credit card numbers, or zip codes (see [Numeric Input Standardization](/docs/guides-numeric-input-standardization--docs)).
doc.numberinput_content_precision: When decimal precision is important, such as for currency or weight, make appropriate settings and communicate expected values to the user.
doc.numberinput_design_intent: NumberInput is a component specialized for entering and fine-tuning numerical values. In addition to users directly entering numbers using the keyboard, it provides a UI where values can be intuitively adjusted using spin buttons (increment/decrement buttons).
doc.numberinput_long_desc: A component that provides numerical input.
doc.numberinput_motion_spin: Numerical values update smoothly when buttons are pressed, communicating the certainty of the operation.
doc.numberinput_scenario_admin: Admin Panel: Specifying the sort order (priority) numerically.
doc.numberinput_scenario_cart: Shopping Cart: Changing the number of items ordered (quantity).
doc.numberinput_title: Field specialized for numeric input with spin buttons
doc.otpinput_a11y_desc: Each digit input is individually focusable with auto-advance. Supports paste for the full code. Uses aria-label to describe the purpose.
doc.otpinput_a11y_label: Communicate to screen readers that each field is requesting individual entry, and make them recognize it as an 'authentication code entry' as a whole group.
doc.otpinput_anatomy_fields: Input Field Sequence: A group of inputs dedicated to a single character, lined up for the specified number of digits.
doc.otpinput_best_practice_1: Auto-focus the first input field when the OTP screen loads to reduce friction.
doc.otpinput_best_practice_2: Support pasting the entire OTP code from clipboard to improve the user experience.
doc.otpinput_choice_alt_when: When the input is a general text field without specific digit-by-digit entry (use Input).
doc.otpinput_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the type of code input needed.
doc.otpinput_choice_self_when: When verifying identity with a one-time passcode (SMS, email, or authenticator app).
doc.otpinput_content_auto: To enhance convenience on mobile devices, it supports automatic completion from SMS (autocomplete="one-time-code").
doc.otpinput_design_intent: OtpInput is a specialized component for entering short authentication codes (usually 4 to 6 digits) used in two-factor authentication, etc. By providing fields categorized for each character, it clarifies the current entry position and reduces entry errors by functioning as a visual guide.
